output: Completed batch 3 tasks T9 and T12. Replaced the ai-timeline-agent scaffold with a Groq tool-use loop that loads timeline state, executes timeline/session tools, persists optimistic-locking writes, handles continue/cancel terminal states, and saves turns on exit. Added the AgentChat sidebar components with session selection, new-conversation flow, auto-scroll, text and voice input, collapsible tool-call cards, thinking state, and kill switch wiring. Verified the touched files with targeted ESLint, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`, and `git diff --check`, and wrote the batch checkpoint.
deviations: Did not execute a live Groq tool-calling request or browser-rendered chat interaction in this batch. Verification for the agent loop and chat panel is based on code inspection plus static checks (`eslint`, `tsc`, and whitespace validation).

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented the `ai-timeline-agent` tool-use loop and prompt template. The function now loads `timelines.config`, `config_version`, `asset_registry`, and `project_id`, builds a system prompt from `view_timeline`, calls Groq with the declared tools, executes timeline/session tools, persists config mutations through `update_timeline_config_versioned`, retries a mutating tool once after reloading on version conflict, checks for cancellation before each LLM turn and tool execution, and persists turns plus terminal status on every exit path. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and `git diff --check` passed for `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/prompts.ts`.

Built the `AgentChat` UI with session selection, new-conversation creation, auto-scroll, scrollable turn history, text input, voice input via `useAgentVoice`, continuation/error notices, a processing spinner, and a kill switch wired to `useCancelSession`. Added `AgentChatMessage` to render user turns right-aligned, assistant turns left-aligned, and tool calls as collapsible cards that reveal tool args and paired tool results. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and `git diff --check` passed for the new component files.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The loop now exits on `message_user`/`done`, after 8 iterations by setting `status='continue'`, on the 50s wall-clock guard by setting `status='continue'`, and when the session row is observed as `cancelled`. It persists `turns`, `status`, and `summary` before returning and also writes an `error` state on unexpected failure. Groq tool calling is wired through `tools` plus `tool_choice: 'auto'`, and the code parses `message.tool_calls`; this was validated statically via typecheck/lint, not with a live Groq API request in this batch.

The chat panel renders user turns with right-aligned primary bubbles and assistant turns with left-aligned card-style bubbles. Tool calls render as collapsed `Collapsible` cards by default and reveal JSON args plus the paired tool result when opened. The red stop button is only rendered when the active session status is `processing` or `continue`. UI verification in this batch is based on component structure plus static checks rather than a live browser render.
